result.markdown:
--- title: "Quickstart" execute: warning: false --- Run your first Josh simulation with joshpy in under 5 minutes. ## 1. Install joshpy ```bash pip install joshpy[all] ``` ## 2. Run a Simulation joshpy's `SweepManager` is the standard way to run simulations — even for single runs. It handles execution, result loading, and DuckDB registry tracking in one workflow: ::: {#6239c796 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} from pathlib import Path from joshpy.jobs import JobConfig from joshpy.sweep import SweepManager from joshpy.cli import JoshCLI from joshpy.jar import JarMode config = JobConfig( source_path=Path("../../examples/quickstart.josh"), simulation="Main", replicates=1, ) manager = ( SweepManager.builder(config) .with_registry(":memory:", experiment_name="quickstart") .with_cli(JoshCLI(josh_jar=JarMode.DEV)) .build() ) results = manager.run() print(f"Success: {results.succeeded}, Failed: {results.failed}") if results.failed > 0: for job, result in results: if not result.success: raise RuntimeError(f"Simulation failed:\n{result.stderr}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Running 1 jobs (1 total replicates) [1/1] Running (local): {} [OK] Completed successfully Completed: 1 succeeded, 0 failed Success: 1, Failed: 0 ``` ::: ::: ## 3. Load and Query Results `load_results()` discovers output CSVs and imports them into the DuckDB registry. Then query with SQL or convenience methods: ::: {#ee793a3d .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} manager.load_results() # What's in the registry? summary = manager.registry.get_data_summary() print(summary) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Loading patch results from: /tmp/quickstart_output.csv Loaded 34782 rows from quickstart_output.csv Results: Jobs in sweep: 1 Jobs with results loaded: 1 Total rows loaded: 34782 Registry Data Summary ======================================== Sessions: 1 Configs: 1 Runs: 1 Rows: 34,782 Variables: averageAge, averageHeight, treeCount Entity types: patch Parameters: (none) Steps: 0 - 10 Replicates: 0 - 0 Spatial extent: lon [-115.39, -114.40], lat [33.40, 33.70] ``` ::: ::: ::: {#a3c61583 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Query results as a DataFrame df = manager.query("averageHeight") df.head(10) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
step mean std ci_low ci_high n_replicates
0 0 0.498945 NaN NaN NaN 1
1 1 0.997634 NaN NaN NaN 1
2 2 1.499646 NaN NaN NaN 1
3 3 1.996777 NaN NaN NaN 1
4 4 2.498130 NaN NaN NaN 1
5 5 2.998429 NaN NaN NaN 1
6 6 3.498851 NaN NaN NaN 1
7 7 4.000108 NaN NaN NaN 1
8 8 4.499050 NaN NaN NaN 1
9 9 4.998641 NaN NaN NaN 1
``` ::: ::: ## 4. Visualize ::: {#fig-quickstart .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} from joshpy.diagnostics import SimulationDiagnostics diag = SimulationDiagnostics(manager.registry) diag.plot_timeseries( "averageHeight", run_hash=manager.job_set.jobs[0].run_hash, title="Tree Growth Over Time", ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![Average tree height increases over time](quickstart_files/figure-html/fig-quickstart-output-1.png){#fig-quickstart-1 width=949 height=564} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ![](quickstart_files/figure-html/fig-quickstart-output-2.png){#fig-quickstart-2 width=949 height=564} ::: ::: ## What Just Happened? The `quickstart.josh` simulation: 1. Creates a **grid** spanning ~30km x 100km in the Mojave Desert 2. Places **10 trees** in each patch (grid cell) 3. Each tree **grows randomly** 0-1 meters per timestep 4. Exports **average height** and **tree count** at each step View the simulation source: ::: {#0505e8e5 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(Path("../../examples/quickstart.josh").read_text()) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # Quickstart simulation - a minimal Josh example # Simulates tree growth over 10 timesteps on a small grid start simulation Main grid.size = 1000 m grid.low = 33.7 degrees latitude, -115.4 degrees longitude grid.high = 34.0 degrees latitude, -116.4 degrees longitude grid.patch = "Default" steps.low = 0 count steps.high = 10 count exportFiles.patch = "file:///tmp/quickstart_output.csv" end simulation start patch Default ForeverTree.init = create 10 count of ForeverTree export.treeCount.step = count(ForeverTree) export.averageAge.step = mean(ForeverTree.age) export.averageHeight.step = mean(ForeverTree.height) end patch start organism ForeverTree age.init = 0 year age.step = prior.age + 1 year height.init = 0 meters height.step = prior.height + sample uniform from 0 meters to 1 meters end organism start unit year alias years alias yr alias yrs end unit ``` ::: ::: ## What's Next? | Feature | Tutorial | |---------|----------| | **Parameter Sweeps** | [SweepManager Workflow](../tutorials/sweep-manager.qmd) | | **Project Organization** | [Project Organization](../tutorials/project-organization.qmd) — directory layout, `.josh.j2` templates, data helpers | | **External Data** | [Preprocessing](../tutorials/preprocessing.qmd) and [Data Sweeps](../tutorials/external-data-sweep.qmd) | | **Analysis** | [Analysis & Visualization](../tutorials/analysis.qmd) | | **Best Practices** | [Registry design, error diagnosis](../best-practices.qmd) | ## Cleanup ::: {#0d1ec25e .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} manager.cleanup() manager.close() ``` :::
